title: RTM Row
description: A single row from the TRACEABILITY.md Requirements Traceability Matrix. Column order: R-ID | Title | Status | Sprint | Code | Tests | Backlog | Notes. The table is identified by having both 'R-ID' AND 'Sprint' in the header. Rows with non-matching R-IDs are silently skipped by parsers.

id.description: R-ID in canonical form. 1-3 digits, optional single lowercase alpha suffix.

title.description: Single-line description of the requirement. Maps to the 'Requirement' column in REQUIREMENTS.md.

status.description: Canonical status. Must be one of the 8 known statuses (case-sensitive, uppercase). Mirrors the status in REQUIREMENTS.md — these must be kept in sync in the same commit.

sprint.description: Sprint folder name (date-prefixed slug, e.g. '2026-04-26_wave13') or empty string if not yet assigned to a sprint.

code.description: Comma-separated paths to primary implementation files relative to repo root. May be empty.

tests.description: Comma-separated paths to test files relative to repo root. May be empty.

backlog.description: Reference to a backlog item ID (e.g. 'BL-01') or empty.

examples.title: EventEmitter bus for async claim lifecycle events
examples.notes: In-process EventEmitter works; AWS SNS/SQS is Tier 2B dependency

examples.title: Push 2FA
examples.notes: Push 2FA adds vendor dependency without strengthening threat model beyond TOTP+WebAuthn
